commit.message:
Skip auto-title when user has set a session title

The PUT /sessions/:id/title endpoint updated the DB but did not mark
the session in sessionHasTitle, so the title service could still fire
and overwrite a user-chosen title. Also add a race-condition guard in
_generate() to check sessionHasTitle after the prompt returns, in case
the user renamed the session while generation was in flight.

Fix bracket-watch: 404 exits 1 (not yet available) not 2 (hard error)

* Fix monobitmap sample: remove poke(PPU_CTRL, 0) that disables NMI

The monobitmap sample had poke(PPU_CTRL, 0) and poke(PPU_MASK, 0) at
startup to disable rendering during VRAM writes. However, poke(PPU_CTRL, 0)
disables NMI, which causes ppu_on_all -> ppu_onoff -> ppu_wait_nmi to hang
forever since NMI never fires to increment STARTUP.

Fix: Remove both poke calls. Rendering is already off at startup (neslib
initializes with PPU_MASK=0), so they were redundant. NMI stays enabled
from the startup code, allowing ppu_wait_nmi to work correctly.

Also: added peek(PPU_STATUS) before scroll writes per review feedback,
added trailing newline to chr_generic.s, added monobitmap screenshot.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test: mock GitLogService in replicate-service tests to prevent flakiness

or execSync timeouts.

deterministic and ~500x faster (5ms vs 2.5s).

fix: remove redundant sub-agent and build invocations from git-workflow agent

Eliminated per-commit Code Review agent invocation and per-commit
npm run build/lint from the Pre-Commit Verification section. These
were causing extremely slow workflows — with 5 atomic commits, the
agent would launch 5 full code-review sub-agents and 5 build/lint
cycles. Now build/lint runs once before starting commits, and the
single pre-PR Code Review gate is preserved.

feat(coc): hide Processes/Skills/Logs/Memory buttons on mobile in TopBar

Use 'hidden md:inline-flex' Tailwind classes on the four tab-navigation
buttons (Processes, Skills, Logs, Memory) so they are hidden on viewports
< 768 px where BottomNav already exposes those tabs. Admin and Theme toggle
remain visible on all screen sizes.

Add tests to TopBar.test.tsx verifying the hidden/md:inline-flex classes
on the four hidden buttons and that admin remains always visible.

Obtain first successful protected strict sign-off run as backend MVP evidence (#541)

* Initial plan

* Fix YAML syntax error in protected-sign-off.yml, add CI23 regression test, update runbook

Root cause: multi-line python3 -c \"...\" with bare newlines at column 0 inside a YAML
literal block scalar (indented at 10 spaces) caused the YAML parser to interpret
\"import sys, json\" as a new mapping key. GitHub marked every workflow run as
'failure' with zero jobs started — all protected-sign-off.yml runs since the workflow
was created were failing for this reason.

Fix:
1. protected-sign-off.yml: Replace 14-line multi-line Python block (lines 435-448)
   with a single-line python3 -c invocation that stays within the YAML block scalar.
   The one-liner is functionally equivalent: extracts WorkflowGovernanceEnabled check
   outcome from the environment check response, returning 'NotChecked' when absent
   and 'ExtractionFailed' via the || fallback when python3 errors.

2. ProtectedSignOffCIWorkflowConfigTests.cs: Add CI23 regression test that reads
   protected-sign-off.yml and asserts no python3 -c invocation contains bare newlines
   at column 0 (which would break YAML parsing). Total: 133 → 134 tests.

3. PROTECTED_SIGN_OFF_RUNBOOK.md:
   - Update test count: 133 → 134, CI1-CI22 → CI1-CI23
   - Update Tier 1 job description: 111 → 134 tests
   - Add 'Optional: External Live-Backend Mode' section documenting SIGNOFF_API_BASE_URL,
     SIGNOFF_TEST_EMAIL, SIGNOFF_TEST_PASSWORD as future optional secrets for external
     backend mode
   - Add 'Workflow YAML Maintenance Rules' section documenting the safe and unsafe patterns
     for embedding Python in GitHub Actions run: blocks

Co-authored-by: ludovit-scholtz <256357527+ludovit-scholtz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: add Regex using directive, clarify shell example in runbook

Co-authored-by: ludovit-scholtz <256357527+ludovit-scholtz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add ProtectedSignOffEndToEndTests - canonical in-process protected strict sign-off evidence (16 tests, E2E01-E2E15)

Addresses product owner feedback that the PR needs actual operational proof of the
backend critical path, not just YAML scaffolding fixes.

ProtectedSignOffEndToEndTests.cs (16 tests) exercises the complete protected sign-off
journey via HTTP through WebApplicationFactory:

E2E01: Full happy path (fixture→env→lifecycle→diag→evidence manifest) succeeds
E2E02: IsLifecycleVerified=true, ReachedStage=Complete (release gate criterion)
E2E03: Environment check returns Ready after fixture provision
E2E03b: Config-check-only returns Ready without requiring fixture prerequisites
E2E04: Fixture provision returns IsProvisioned=true with correct issuer ID
E2E05: Diagnostics returns IsOperational=true, no configuration failures
E2E06: Evidence manifest JSON-serializable with all required fields populated
E2E07: Determinism proof — identical results across 3 independent factory instances
E2E08: Wrong-password returns 401, distinguishable from backend unavailability (503)
E2E09: Failed lifecycle attributes failure to specific stage with UserGuidance
E2E10: Missing required config → Misconfigured status (fail-closed, not Ready)
E2E11: All 6 stages in correct order with Verified outcomes in happy path
E2E12: All Verified stages have null UserGuidance (clean evidence artifact)
E2E13: CorrelationId echoed across all 4 API endpoints
E2E14: WorkflowGovernanceEnabled check is observable in environment check response
E2E15: Two concurrent users each complete the full journey independently

PROTECTED_SIGN_OFF_RUNBOOK.md:
- Add 'Canonical In-Process Protected Sign-Off Evidence' section with per-test evidence table
- Add mapping from E2E tests to Tier 2 CI workflow steps
- Update test count: 134 → 150 (six test classes)

Total ProtectedSignOff tests: 150 (all passing)

     local auth/session types, eliminating double-schema maintenance

     lookupBarcode→scanBarcode, wrapped mutations in payload types, removed
     userId from records query, added bidirectional pagination (last/before)

* fix: update schema handling in Dockerfile and package.json for Relay compatibility

* fix(ci): generate Relay schema statically from source files at build time

   The frontend Docker build was failing because prebuild tried to fetch the
   stitched schema from a live BFF (localhost:3001), which doesn't exist inside
   a build container.

   Replace get-graphql-schema with a small build script
   (packages/frontend/scripts/generate-schema.mjs) that merges the two static
   source files directly:
   - Parses packages/backend/src/schema.graphql
   - Strips backend-internal type definitions overridden by the BFF
     (Tenant, TenantType, TenantRole, FeatureFlags)
   - Merges in packages/bff/src/schema.graphql extensions
   - Writes packages/frontend/schema.graphql for the Relay compiler

   No live server, no committed artifact. When the backend schema changes,
   prebuild regenerates everything automatically. The Dockerfile is updated
   to COPY both source .graphql files into the build context.

* fix(bff): resolve Unknown type Tenant startup crash in stitched schema

Root cause: stripping Tenant from the backend SDL before buildASTSchema left
createTenant: Tenant! as a dangling reference with assumeValidSDL. When
stitchSchemas walked the backend mutation type's fields, the unresolvable
Tenant reference caused new GraphQLSchema() to throw Unknown type: Tenant.

Fix:
- Build the full, valid backend schema (no SDL stripping, no assumeValidSDL)
- Use RenameTypes(Tenant → BackendTenant) on the remote subschema so the
  backend's internal Tenant (tenantId/databaseName/…) doesn't conflict with
  the BFF's client-facing Tenant (id/name/type/role)
- Remove TenantType/TenantRole from bff/src/schema.graphql — they are
  identical enums defined in the backend schema and arrive via stitching
- Update generate-schema.mjs BFF_OWNED to only filter Tenant + FeatureFlags;
  TenantType/TenantRole now come from the backend SDL in the frontend schema

Verified locally: schema stitches cleanly, all 10 BFF tests pass, frontend
schema.graphql regenerates to 551 lines with correct Tenant/TenantType/TenantRole.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(demoserver): correct backend schema path for BFF stitching

After the schema-stitching refactor, the BFF resolves the backend
schema file at startup via resolveBackendSchemaPath(). In the
demo-server container the BFF runs from packages/bff/dist/graphql/,
so candidate path 1 resolves to packages/backend/src/schema.graphql.

The Dockerfile was copying the file to packages/backend/dist/schema.graphql
(neither candidate path), which would cause a startup crash with
'Backend schema not found'.

Fix: copy to packages/backend/src/schema.graphql to match the resolved path.

* Fix transpiler bug: include pusha/pushax for programs using decsp4

Programs that use decsp4 (oam_spr with 4+ args) could also need
pusha/pushax subroutines for byte array/string parameter passing
and user method prologues. Previously, pusha/pushax were excluded
whenever decsp4 was used (unless scroll/split were also present),
causing JSR pusha/pushax to resolve to address  at runtime.

Fix: scan emitted program blocks for JSR pusha/pushax label
references after code generation completes. If found, add them
to UsedMethods so Program6502 includes the subroutines in ROM.
This is done post-generation because block buffering can remove
instructions (e.g., EmitOamSprDecsp4 replaces buffered JSR pusha
with inline decsp4 code), so tracking during EmitJSR would
produce false positives.

Also adds missing chr_generic.s to siegegame sample and updates
the siegegame verified.bin snapshot.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Recompile siegegame test DLLs locally for cross-platform consistency

The Copilot agent compiled the original DLLs on a different platform,
which may produce different metadata ordering. Recompile locally with
the same .NET 10 SDK to ensure consistent transpiler output across
Windows, macOS, and Linux.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix byte[] method params and string label collisions in user methods

Two transpiler bugs that affected the siegegame sample:

1. byte[] parameters to user-defined methods lost their high byte.
   The method prologue used pusha (1-byte push) for all params, but
   byte[] params are 16-bit pointers needing pushax (2-byte push).
   - Parse ELEMENT_TYPE_SZARRAY from method signatures to detect byte[] params
   - Use pushax in prologue for byte[] last param
   - Load both lo/hi bytes in WriteLdarg for byte[] params
   - Correct epilogue stack cleanup (2 bytes per byte[] param)

2. String labels collided across user methods. Each method writer
   generated string_0, string_1, etc. independently. On merge,
   MergeStringTableEntry skipped duplicate labels, so later methods'
   strings silently pointed to earlier methods' string data.
   - Add StringLabelStartIndex (like ByteArrayLabelStartIndex)
   - Offset string labels per method writer to prevent collisions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Remove incorrect PAD_* constants, use PAD enum values directly
- Fix 'PRESS START TO PLAY' text to match PAD.A button check
- Remove false claim about string.Length in header comment
- Batch ppu_off/ppu_on_all in gameplay loop instead of per-tile
  read/write (avoids 2 NMI waits per vram_read/write call)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix sprite rendering: OAM offset tracking and transpiler bugs

Three transpiler fixes for correct sprite rendering in siegegame:

1. incsp1 BNE offset fix (BuiltInSubroutines.cs): Changed BNE(3) to
   BNE(2) so the branch lands on RTS instead of jumping past it into
   ROM data, which caused illegal opcode crashes.

2. _lastByteArrayLabel leak fix (IL2NESWriter.ILDispatch.cs): Clear
   the label after consumption in the default Call handler so palette
   byte array addresses don't leak into subsequent stsfld operations,
   corrupting function arguments.

3. oam_spr return value tracking (IL2NESWriter.OamSprites.cs): Set
   _runtimeValueInA = true after JSR oam_spr so that
   oam_off = oam_spr(...) correctly emits STA to OAM_OFF.

Game code changes (siegegame/Program.cs):
- Pre-compute oam_spr arguments into globals (tempNX/tempNY) to avoid
  the array-element-in-oam_spr argument resolution bug
- Use oam_off = oam_spr(..., oam_off) pattern for proper OAM offset
  tracking so each sprite gets its own OAM slot
- Custom CHR tiles for wall, castle, enemy, cursor, and floor

Verified: Mesen2 confirms cursor sprite at (112,112) tile  and
enemy sprite at separate OAM slot with correct tile . OAM_OFF
increments by 4 per sprite. All 442 tests pass.
